segments.text: This is the single most important skill
for 2026 and I will break down exactly
how you can get a job in this role. Most
students misunderstand the product
manager role. They think it's [music]
either a tech job or a management job.
That's not true. It's about
decision-making. PMs sit at the
intersection of business, tech, and
users. And here's how you can become
one. Start by learning how PMs [music]
think. Read Inspired by Marty Kagan.
Then watch Exponent Product Management
mock interviews on YouTube. Next, build
basic data skills. [music] Learn SQL for
data analysis via free code camp on
YouTube. After that, learn Excel and the
fundamentals of metrics and AB testing.
You don't need to code apps. You need to
understand data. Then do real work. Do a
product tearown. Pick apps like Swiggy
or WhatsApp. Identify one problem and
write a one-page solution with success
metrics. Publish it on LinkedIn or
Medium. [music] Entry-level associate
PMs in India start around 8 to 15 LPA.
With strong impact, this can cross 30 to
50 LPA [music]
in a few years. Share this with a friend
and follow Full Disclosure for more
insights on careers.
